_meta.description: Visitor experience moments, tour types, and highlights displayed on the website

whenToEdit: Adding a new visitor experience highlight
Changing what's included in a visit
Adding seasonal or temporary tour options
staffInstructions.howToEdit: On GitHub: Navigate to data/experiences.json > Click pencil icon > Make changes > Click 'Commit changes'
writingTips: Use active, engaging verbs (Walk, Meet, Touch, Discover)
Focus on what the visitor DOES, not what we have
Avoid jargon - write for first-time visitors

experienceMoments.description: The 'What You'll Experience' section - key moments that make a visit special

fields.numeral: Roman numeral (I, II, III, IV) - keeps the heritage aesthetic
fields.title: Short action phrase (3-5 words) starting with a verb
fields.description: One sentence explaining what this experience means to the visitor
fields.icon: Icon name (footprints, users, hammer, scroll, building, theater)
fields.category: Grouping for filtering - immersion, interpretation, hands-on, or education

tourTypes.description: Different tour options available to visitors

fields.name: Display name for the tour
fields.description: One sentence about what happens on this tour
fields.included: true if included with admission, false if extra

highlights.format: Simple strings - keep each under 40 characters

experienceMoments.keepToFour: Exactly 4 moments works best visually with Roman numerals I-IV
experienceMoments.verbFirst: Titles should start with action verbs (Stand, Meet, Touch, Discover)
experienceMoments.visitorFocused: Write from the visitor's perspective, not the museum's

tourTypes.honestDurations: Use ranges to set realistic expectations
tourTypes.accessibilityTransparency: Be honest about physical requirements - helps visitors plan
tourTypes.includedMatters: Mark 'included: true' for anything covered by general admission

iconNames.note: Icons must match Lucide icon library - ask developer to add new icons

newExperienceMoment.description: Copy this to add a new visitor experience highlight

example.title: Verb the Subject
example.description: One sentence describing what the visitor will do or feel during this experience.

newTourType.description: Copy this to add a new tour option

example.name: Your Tour Name
example.description: Brief description of what happens on this tour

newVisitInclude.description: Copy this to add a new 'included with admission' feature

example.title: Feature Name
example.description: Brief description of this included feature

newHighlight.description: Copy this format for bullet-point highlights

experienceCategories.immersion: Physical/spatial experiences (walking grounds, being in historic spaces)

goodExamples: Stand on Historic Ground - active, physical, memorable
Meet the Settlers - personal, engaging, promises interaction
Touch the Past - sensory, tangible, unique
avoidExamples: Learn About History - too generic, passive
See Our Collection - museum-focused, not visitor-focused
Rocky Mount Has Buildings - not experiential

_aiRules.description: Rules for AI assistants editing this file
beforeEditing: ALWAYS verify changes with user before committing
ALWAYS follow _meta.writingGuidelines for tone and style
prohibited: Do NOT make changes without user awareness
Do NOT use icons not listed in _meta.rules.iconNames.available
Do NOT exceed 4 experienceMoments (visual design constraint)
Do NOT use passive or museum-centric language
fileSpecific: experienceMoments: Exactly 4 items with Roman numerals I-IV, verb-first titles
tourTypes: Use honest duration ranges and accurate accessibility info
visitIncludes: Only features covered by standard admission
highlights: Keep each under 40 characters for quick scanning
Use templates in _meta.templates when adding new items

validation.experienceMoments: Must have exactly 4 items with unique Roman numerals I-IV
validation.icons: Must be from: footprints, users, hammer, scroll, building, theater, home, flag
validation.categories: Must be: immersion, interpretation, hands-on, or education

writingRules: Titles start with action verbs (Stand, Meet, Touch, Discover)
Write from visitor perspective, not museum perspective
Avoid jargon - write for first-time visitors
_aiRules.whenUnsure: Ask the user for clarification, especially about accessibility claims or tour availability

experienceMoments.title: Stand on Historic Ground
experienceMoments.description: Walk the same grounds where Governor Blount governed the Southwest Territory and shaped the future state of Tennessee.

experienceMoments.title: Meet the Settlers
experienceMoments.description: Costumed interpreters bring the Cobb family and their world to life through first-person storytelling.

experienceMoments.title: Touch the Past
experienceMoments.description: Experience authentic 18th-century crafts, from blacksmithing to hearth cooking, using period tools and techniques.

experienceMoments.title: Discover the Evidence
experienceMoments.description: Examine original documents, letters, and artifacts from the territorial period in our museum galleries.

tourTypes.name: Museum Gallery Tour
tourTypes.description: Self-guided exploration of exhibits and artifacts

tourTypes.name: Historic Site Tour
tourTypes.description: Guided tour of original log structures with costumed interpreters

tourTypes.name: Twilight Tour
tourTypes.description: Evening candlelit tour with enhanced storytelling

visitIncludes.title: Guided Tour
visitIncludes.description: Expert-led tour of historic buildings with costumed interpreters

visitIncludes.title: Historic Buildings
visitIncludes.description: Access to all original 18th-century log structures on site

visitIncludes.title: Museum & Gift Shop
visitIncludes.description: Exhibits, artifacts, and curated heritage items

highlights: Historic 1820s log buildings on 1770s homestead site
